_note: Terminal economy sweep, done_sentinel for W9 (orchestrator STEP-0 derived-state). Operator-RESCOPED 2026-06-11: Layer-2 (ECONOMY-AUDIT, LLM) surfaced 15 INHERITED findings on the 4 touched prompts; none from the W9 edit (the _step-runner C4 'just' drop audited clean). Resolving all 15 = re-authoring 3 content-untouched shipped prompts + orchestrator, which overlaps the deferred W10 full-sweep + risks regressing W6/W7-verified prompts. Operator chose: CLOSE W9 (lint 44/44 clean + _step-runner fixed + zero-new-L2-debt + both-directions proof) and ROLL the 15 inherited findings into a new W10-ECON-SWEEP-FULL (full 44-prompt L2 reconcile, corpus-wide auditor calibration). Disk = source of truth (D20); this report is the W9 sentinel, not a tracker.

scope.lint_layer1: whole corpus (44 prompts) — deterministic, ran in full
scope.audit_layer2: 4 touched prompts only (SEQUENCE, SLICE-EXTRACT, _orchestrator, _step-runner); full 44-prompt L2 sweep -> W10
scope.fix_path: lint mis-fires = controller-direct calibration (P3, like W7); genuine prompt debt = IMPLEMENT + clean-room (operator-chosen)

resolution.root: lint mis-fire — regex matched 'PR4' on L65 (canon-bundle enum 'AB1-AB6 + PR1-PR4 + caveman block', a load-bearing REFERENCE naming what IMPLEMENT authors against) and L110 (RULES' own register self-rule for the shipped prompt) = two DISTINCT facts at two homes, not a duplicated reminder
resolution.fix: tools/economy-lint/lint.mjs C8: exclude lines where PR4 is inside a canon-bundle range/list (bundleRef = /PR\d[–—-]PR4|AB\d[^.]*PR\d/). Analog of routes_to:canon — fix the checker, not the artifact (ledger pre-flagged this W7).
resolution.regression_test: tools/economy-lint/selftest.mjs 'C8-calibration' (bundle-ref + single reminder must NOT trip C8) + original C8 defect still blocks

resolution.root: lint mis-fire (pure) — C6 counted token overlap from SUCCESS-exit Stop bullets (greenfield/feature-add -> write 05/08), which a Stop MUST name (PR stop-contract). Mode/path/verdict vocabulary overlaps the mode-keyed escapes WITHOUT re-stating any guard PREDICATE. The dependency-defect mnemonic sits on a write-and-route (non-HALT) line. No predicate on any HALT line.
resolution.fix: tools/economy-lint/lint.mjs C6: build stopTok only from FAILURE-exit lines (/(?<!not[- ])\bHALT\b|write nothing/), excluding success/write-and-continue exits + negated 'not-HALT' mentions. A guard's wrong home is a HALT/write-nothing line; that is where a re-enumeration lives.
resolution.regression_test: tools/economy-lint/selftest.mjs 'C6-calibration' (mode-keyed success exits must NOT trip C6) + original C6 defect (predicates copied onto HALT lines) still blocks

resolution.root: lint mis-fire (dominant) — same multi-exit collision; the one genuine residue (HALT-line parenthetical re-listing 'no frozen aPRD, missing/invalid lock, unplaybooked class') tripped only 1 guard >=3 overlap (below the >=2 fire threshold) post-calibration. Minor AB2 residue is sub-threshold + arguably an acceptable mnemonic; routed to W10 for the corpus-wide call.
resolution.fix: same C6 calibration as SEQUENCE; SLICE-EXTRACT goes clean with calibration alone (no prompt edit). Minor HALT-line parenthetical catalogued in W10.

resolution.root: GENUINE prompt debt — filler 'just' in 'The operator just pasted one prompt into you'
resolution.fix: IMPLEMENT authored scratch (drop 'just', all else byte-identical) -> lint clean -> L2 audit clean on the changed line -> promoted prompts/_step-runner.md. Verify: 1-line diff confirmed; behavioral surface (Hard rules/Register/Final reply) byte-identical -> clean-room behavior provably unchanged.
